reviewer.name: Architecture Enforcement Reviewer
reviewer.summary:
PR #398 delivered Phase 1 correctly: BiostatisticsConfig (Pydantic), 9 dataclasses in biostatistics_types.py, default.yaml with 8 metrics + ROPE values. 20 unit tests passing. Config is clean — no Prefect imports, no external deps, pure Python types.
CRITICAL GAP: The original plan (v1 task 8.1) says "Uses _prefect_compat.py for graceful degradation without Prefect." This MUST be removed. The biostatistics flow has NO valid use case without Prefect. Unlike training (which pytest needs to mock), biostatistics is a pure-compute flow that reads DuckDB and produces artifacts. If Prefect is not installed, the flow should FAIL LOUDLY, not silently degrade.
The flow must enforce: 1. Docker context check (/.dockerenv OR MINIVESS_TESTING=1 for pytest) 2. Prefect must be importable (no compat fallback) 3. All output paths must be volume-mounted (not /tmp, not repo-relative) 4. Input mlruns_dir must be volume-mounted (read-only)

findings.finding: Add _require_docker_context() guard at flow entry point. Check for /.dockerenv file. Escape hatch: MINIVESS_TESTING=1 (for pytest only, NOT for "quick local runs").
All file outputs must use config.output_dir which maps to a Docker volume mount (/app/outputs/biostatistics). No tempfile, no repo paths.
config.mlruns_dir must map to /app/mlruns (Docker volume, read-only). The flow reads mlruns but never writes to them.

reviewer.name: Statistical Methodology Reviewer
reviewer.summary:
The statistical methodology from v1 plan is sound. Key decisions remain valid: - BCa bootstrap (N=70 per-volume) as PRIMARY inference (not fold-level N=3) - Two-tier MCC: Holm for primary metric, BH-FDR for secondary - Bayesian signed-rank with ROPE (baycomp) for equivalence testing - ICC(2,1) for cross-fold consistency (not Bland-Altman) - Three effect sizes: Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, VDA
EXISTING CODE TO REUSE (from comparison.py + ci.py): - paired_bootstrap_test() — works, keep as-is - bca_bootstrap_ci() — works, keep as-is - cohens_d() — works, keep as-is - holm_bonferroni_correction() — works, keep as-is
MISSING IMPLEMENTATIONS (need new code or library calls): - cliffs_delta() — trivial formula, 5 lines - vargha_delaney_a() — trivial, derived from Cliff's delta - BH-FDR correction — scipy.stats.false_discovery_control or manual - Friedman + Nemenyi — scipy + scikit-posthocs - ICC(2,1) — pingouin.intraclass_corr - Bayesian signed-rank — baycomp.SignedRankTest.probs
CI AVERAGING BUG (comparison.py _summarise_metric): The bug where CI endpoints are averaged (mean of lower bounds, mean of upper bounds) instead of computing a pooled bootstrap is a known issue. Fix in Phase 3.

findings.finding: Library-first: Use scipy.stats.wilcoxon (not custom), scipy.stats.friedmanchisquare (not custom), pingouin.intraclass_corr (not custom). Only write custom code for Cliff's delta (5 lines) and VDA (1 line) since no established library function exactly matches the paired formulation needed here.

Seed determinism: ALL random operations (bootstrap, Bayesian sampling) must use numpy.random.Generator(numpy.random.PCG64(seed)) — NOT numpy.random.seed(). This ensures thread-safety and reproducibility.
BH-FDR: Use statsmodels.stats.multitest.multipletests(method='fdr_bh') which is more battle-tested than scipy.stats.false_discovery_control (new in scipy 1.11).

reviewer.name: Docker-Prefect Compliance Reviewer
reviewer.summary:
The deployment infrastructure for biostatistics flow does NOT exist yet: - No Dockerfile.biostatistics - No biostatistics service in docker-compose.flows.yml - No deployment config in deployments.yaml - No entry in FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP or FLOW_IMAGE_MAP
The EXISTING Docker infrastructure (docker-compose.flows.yml) is well-structured with 9 services, proper volume mounts, GPU reservations. The biostatistics flow is CPU-only, so it's simpler than training.
KEY LEARNING from metalearning: The infrastructure must be built BEFORE the flow code, not after. If the Dockerfile doesn't exist when the flow is written, the temptation to "just run it locally" is overwhelming.
Required Docker mounts for biostatistics: - /app/mlruns:ro (input: read existing runs) - /app/outputs/biostatistics (output: DuckDB, figures, tables, lineage) - /app/configs:ro (input: biostatistics config YAML)
No GPU needed. No /tmp. No repo-relative paths.

findings.finding: Build Dockerfile.biostatistics and docker-compose service FIRST (Phase 0), before any flow code. This inverts the original plan which had Docker in Phase 9. Rationale: Docker must exist before code, or the code will be written for local execution and retrofitted for Docker (the exact pattern that caused 6 fuckups).

docker-compose.flows.yml biostatistics service must have: - NO gpu device reservation (CPU-only flow) - volumes: mlruns_data:/app/mlruns:ro, biostat_output:/app/outputs/biostatistics, configs_biostatistics:/app/configs/biostatistics:ro - environment: PREFECT_API_URL, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/app/mlruns
Add biostatistics to deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml with: work_pool: cpu-pool, image: minivess-biostatistics:latest
The run command is ALWAYS: docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run biostatistics NEVER: uv run python -c "from ... import biostatistics_flow; ..."

reviewer.name: Testing Strategy Reviewer
reviewer.summary:
The test strategy must operate at TWO levels: 1. DETERMINISTIC (pre-commit hooks + CI tests): Cannot be ignored by AI 2. STOCHASTIC (CLAUDE.md rules + MEMORY.md): Can be ignored under pressure
From metalearning: The AI has bypassed stochastic guardrails 6 times in 6 days. Documentation-only safeguards are INSUFFICIENT. Every critical invariant must have a deterministic test that fails the build.
CRITICAL TESTS NEEDED: A. AST test: biostatistics_flow.py imports from prefect, NOT _prefect_compat B. AST test: biostatistics_flow.py calls _require_docker_context() at entry C. No test file imports biostatistics_flow and runs it without MINIVESS_TESTING=1 D. Pre-commit hook: no .py file in src/ does "from.*_prefect_compat import" for biostatistics (the compat layer is banned for this flow) E. Docker-compose test: biostatistics service exists with correct volumes F. Integration test: full flow with synthetic mlruns in Docker (or MINIVESS_TESTING=1)

findings.finding: Test that biostatistics_flow.py does NOT import from _prefect_compat. This is a deterministic AST test that prevents the compat-layer escape hatch. Pattern: ast.parse the file, walk all Import/ImportFrom nodes, assert none reference "_prefect_compat".

Test that biostatistics_flow.py contains a call to _require_docker_context() or equivalent Docker gate. AST test: find all Call nodes in the flow function, verify at least one calls a function with "docker" or "container" in its name.

Integration test must create synthetic mlruns directory structure (4 conditions x 3 folds x 70 volumes), run the full flow with MINIVESS_TESTING=1, and verify ALL output artifacts: - biostatistics.duckdb exists and has 7 tables - 8+ figure PNG files exist - 8+ JSON sidecar files exist with correct schema - 5+ LaTeX table files exist - lineage.json exists with correct fingerprint - MLflow run was created in minivess_biostatistics experiment

Pre-commit hook: Scan all .py files in src/minivess/orchestration/flows/ for "_prefect_compat" imports. If found in biostatistics_flow.py, REJECT. Other flows may still use compat layer (legacy), but biostatistics must not.

reviewer.name: Anti-Shortcut Guardrails Reviewer
reviewer.summary:
The meta-pattern across 6 fuckups is: "expediency over architecture." The AI correctly reads the rules, then bypasses them when friction increases. The only proven defense is making the wrong path physically impossible.
FOR BIOSTATISTICS FLOW, the shortcut temptations are: 1. "Let me just run the stats locally with uv run python..." — Docker is slow to build 2. "Let me use _prefect_compat.py so it works without Prefect server..." — Server setup 3. "Let me save figures to ./outputs/ in the repo..." — No volume mount needed 4. "Let me skip the Docker build since it's CPU-only..." — Simpler without Docker 5. "Let me create scripts/run_biostatistics.py for quick iteration..." — RULE #17
GUARDRAILS (deterministic, code-level): G1: biostatistics_flow.py has NO compat-layer import (AST test) G2: biostatistics_flow.py calls Docker context check (AST test) G3: No scripts/run_biostatistics*.py exists (Glob test) G4: No PREFECT_DISABLED in any biostatistics-related shell script (grep test) G5: docker-compose.flows.yml has biostatistics service (YAML parse test) G6: All output paths in flow use config.output_dir (not hardcoded paths) G7: Pre-commit hook blocks _prefect_compat import in biostatistics_flow.py
GUARDRAILS (stochastic, documentation-level): S1: CLAUDE.md rule explicitly mentioning biostatistics flow Docker requirement S2: MEMORY.md entry about this plan S3: Metalearning doc if any shortcut is attempted

findings.finding: Create test_biostatistics_no_shortcuts.py with 7 deterministic tests: 1. test_no_compat_layer_import 2. test_docker_context_check_present 3. test_no_standalone_script_exists 4. test_no_prefect_disabled_in_shell_scripts 5. test_docker_compose_has_biostatistics_service 6. test_output_paths_use_config_not_hardcoded 7. test_no_tmp_or_tempfile_usage These tests run in CI and pre-commit. They are DETERMINISTIC and cannot be bypassed without modifying the test file itself (which would be visible in PR).

Do NOT create scripts/run_biostatistics.py or any standalone entry point. The ONLY entry points are: 1. prefect deployment run 'biostatistics-flow/default' --params '{...}' 2. docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run biostatistics 3. pytest (with MINIVESS_TESTING=1, for test suite only)
The Dockerfile.biostatistics must be built and tested BEFORE any flow code is written. This prevents the "just run it locally for now" temptation.

completed-work.item: configs/biostatistics/default.yaml — Default config with 8 metrics + ROPE

changes-from-v1.change: REMOVED: "_prefect_compat.py for graceful degradation" from flow assembly (v1 task 8.1). REPLACED WITH: Direct import from prefect package. ImportError = correct failure.

MOVED: Docker setup from Phase 9 (last) to Phase 0 (first). RATIONALE: Build infrastructure before code, not after. If Docker doesn't exist when flow code is written, the code will be written for local execution.
ADDED: Phase 0 — Anti-Shortcut Guardrails (deterministic tests + pre-commit hooks). These tests run BEFORE any implementation and block shortcuts at the code level.
REMOVED: Any mention of scripts/run_biostatistics.py or standalone entry points. The ONLY entry points are Prefect deployment and Docker Compose.
REMOVED: "test_flow_works_without_prefect (compat mode)" from v1 task 8.1 tests. There is NO compat mode. The flow requires Prefect. Period.
CHANGED: BH-FDR implementation from scipy.stats.false_discovery_control to statsmodels.stats.multitest.multipletests(method='fdr_bh') for battle-tested reliability.
ADDED: Seed determinism requirement — numpy.random.Generator(PCG64(seed)) instead of numpy.random.seed(). Thread-safe reproducibility.

phase.name: Docker Infrastructure and Anti-Shortcut Guardrails
phase.description:
Build the Docker container and deterministic guardrail tests BEFORE any flow code. This inverts the v1 plan which had Docker last (Phase 9). The guardrail tests will initially FAIL (RED phase) and will turn GREEN as subsequent phases implement the flow. This ensures the architectural constraints are tested from day one.
RATIONALE (from metalearning-2026-03-07): "If Docker doesn't exist when flow code is written, the code will be written for local execution and retrofitted for Docker."

task.name: Dockerfile.biostatistics + docker-compose service
task.description:
CPU-only Dockerfile for biostatistics flow: - FROM minivess-base:latest - No GPU, no CUDA - Install: duckdb, scikit-posthocs, pingouin, baycomp, ptitprince, dabest, statsmodels, matplotlib, seaborn - ENTRYPOINT: prefect flow run (NOT python -c) - WORKDIR: /app
docker-compose.flows.yml biostatistics service: - build: context ../, dockerfile deployment/docker/Dockerfile.biostatistics - volumes: - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns:ro - biostat_output:/app/outputs/biostatistics - configs_splits:/app/configs:ro - environment: - PREFECT_API_URL=${PREFECT_API_URL:-http://prefect-server:4200/api} - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=/app/mlruns - NO deploy.resources.reservations.devices (no GPU) - depends_on: [prefect-server] (if orchestrated)
Extend test_docker_compose_volumes.py: - test_biostatistics_service_exists - test_biostatistics_has_mlruns_volume_readonly - test_biostatistics_has_output_volume - test_biostatistics_has_no_gpu

task.name: Deployment config (Prefect + image map)
task.description:
Add biostatistics to: 1. FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP: "biostatistics" -> "cpu-pool" 2. FLOW_IMAGE_MAP: "biostatistics" -> "minivess-biostatistics:latest" 3. deployments.yaml: biostatistics deployment config
Update existing tests to expect the new flow.

task.name: Anti-shortcut guardrail tests (RED — will fail initially)
task.description:
Deterministic tests that enforce architectural constraints. These tests check the STRUCTURE of the codebase, not runtime behavior. They will initially fail (file doesn't exist yet) and turn green as the flow is implemented.
7 guardrail tests: 1. test_no_compat_layer_import — AST-parse biostatistics_flow.py, verify no ImportFrom node references "_prefect_compat". Direct prefect imports only. 2. test_docker_context_check_present — AST-parse biostatistics_flow.py, find a function call to _require_docker_context or _require_container_context. 3. test_no_standalone_script_exists — Glob for scripts/*biostatistic* and scripts/*biostats*. Assert empty. No standalone entry points allowed. 4. test_no_prefect_disabled_in_biostatistics_scripts — grep all .sh files for PREFECT_DISABLED + biostatistics. Assert no matches. 5. test_docker_compose_has_biostatistics_service — Parse docker-compose.flows.yml, assert "biostatistics" key exists in services. 6. test_no_tmp_or_tempfile_usage — AST-parse all biostatistics_*.py files, verify no import of tempfile, no string "/tmp" in source. 7. test_flow_imports_real_prefect — AST-parse biostatistics_flow.py, verify at least one ImportFrom with module="prefect".
IMPORTANT: Tests that check biostatistics_flow.py will be SKIPPED (not failed) until the file exists. Use pytest.importorskip or Path.exists() guard. Once the file exists, they MUST pass.

task.name: Add biostatistics dependencies to pyproject.toml
task.description:
Add new dependencies for biostatistics flow: - scikit-posthocs>=0.11 (Nemenyi, CD diagrams) - pingouin>=0.5 (ICC, effect sizes) - baycomp>=1.0 (Bayesian signed-rank with ROPE) - ptitprince>=0.2 (raincloud plots) - dabest>=2024.1 (DABEST estimation plots — check PyPI for latest) - statsmodels>=0.14 (BH-FDR via multipletests)
Run uv sync to verify all install correctly. Run uv run python -c "import scikit_posthocs; import pingouin; ..." to verify.

phase.name: Config and Data Types
phase.description: COMPLETE from PR #398. No work needed. - BiostatisticsConfig Pydantic model (13 tests passing) - 9 dataclasses in biostatistics_types.py (7 tests passing) - configs/biostatistics/default.yaml

phase.name: DuckDB Foundation
phase.description: Multi-experiment DuckDB extraction with per-volume metrics table, source run discovery, and completeness validation. All functions are PURE (no Prefect, no Docker dependency) — they operate on paths.

task.name: Extended DuckDB schema + extraction
task.description: build_biostatistics_duckdb(manifest, mlruns_dir, output_path) -> Path. Schema: 7 tables (runs, params, eval_metrics, training_metrics, champion_tags, per_volume_metrics, ensemble_members). New tables: per_volume_metrics (run_id, fold_id, volume_id, metric_name, metric_value), ensemble_members (ensemble_run_id, member_run_id). Add experiment_name + experiment_id columns to runs table. Full rebuild every time (small dataset: ~840 metric rows). Export Parquet alongside DuckDB for portability. Reuse existing duckdb_extraction.py patterns (str.partition, no regex).
task.tests: - test_schema_has_7_tables - test_runs_table_has_experiment_columns - test_per_volume_metrics_table_exists - test_parquet_export_alongside_duckdb - test_extraction_from_mock_mlruns - test_idempotent_rebuild

task.name: Source run discovery
task.description: discover_source_runs(mlruns_dir, experiment_names) -> SourceRunManifest. Reads mlruns filesystem, finds all FINISHED runs in specified experiments. Returns SourceRunManifest with SHA256 fingerprint of sorted run IDs.

task.name: Source completeness validation
task.description: validate_source_completeness(manifest, min_folds, min_conditions) -> ValidationResult. Checks: all conditions have >= min_folds, no FAILED/RUNNING runs, at least min_conditions conditions, per-volume metrics exist. Raises BiostatisticsValidationError on critical failures (NOT silent).

phase.name: Statistical Engine
phase.description: Core statistical computations. All functions are PURE — they read DuckDB and return result dataclasses. Zero Prefect dependency. Zero Docker dependency. Config-driven (metric names, loss names from BiostatisticsConfig, never hardcoded).

task.name: Cliff's delta + VDA + CI averaging fix
task.description: 1. Fix _summarise_metric() CI averaging bug — replace mean-of-endpoints with pooled BCa bootstrap. 2. Add cliffs_delta(a, b) -> float. Formula: np.mean(np.sign(a[:, None] - b[None, :])) Thresholds: |d| < 0.147 negligible, < 0.33 small, < 0.474 medium, else large. 3. Add vargha_delaney_a(a, b) -> float. VDA = (cliffs_delta + 1) / 2. 4. Add interpret_cliffs_delta(d) and interpret_vda(a) -> str.

task.name: Pairwise comparisons from DuckDB
task.description: compute_pairwise_comparisons(db_path, config) -> list[PairwiseResult]. For each metric x condition pair: 1. Query per-volume metrics from DuckDB 2. BCa bootstrap CI (B=config.n_bootstrap, seed via PCG64) 3. Wilcoxon signed-rank test (scipy.stats.wilcoxon) 4. Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, VDA 5. Two-tier MCC: Holm for primary_metric, BH-FDR for secondary (via statsmodels.stats.multitest.multipletests) Reuses existing paired_bootstrap_test() + bca_bootstrap_ci() + cohens_d().

task.name: Bayesian signed-rank with ROPE
task.description: compute_bayesian_comparisons(db_path, config) -> list updated PairwiseResult. For each condition pair x primary metric: 1. baycomp.SignedRankTest.probs(scores_A, scores_B, rope=config.rope) 2. Returns P(A>B), P(rope), P(B>A) 3. Updates PairwiseResult.bayesian_left/rope/right Graceful skip if baycomp not installed (optional enhancement, NOT a compat hack). Difference from compat layer: this is a SINGLE OPTIONAL computation within a required flow, not the entire flow being optional.

task.name: Variance decomposition (Friedman + ICC)
task.description: compute_variance_decomposition(db_path, config) -> list[VarianceDecompositionResult]. For each metric: 1. Friedman test (scipy.stats.friedmanchisquare) 2. Nemenyi post-hoc (scikit_posthocs.posthoc_nemenyi_friedman) if significant 3. ICC(2,1) via pingouin.intraclass_corr Set power_caveat=True when K=3 (always, for this dataset).

task.name: Multi-metric ranking and champion confirmation
task.description: compute_rankings(db_path, pairwise_results, config) -> list[RankingResult]. 1. Per-metric ranking (lower rank = better) 2. Mean rank across metrics (Demsar rank-then-aggregate) 3. Critical Difference value (from scikit_posthocs) 4. Champion confirmation: does evidence support each champion category? Reuses rank_then_aggregate() from champion_tagger.py.

phase.name: Figure Generation
phase.description: Publication-quality figures with JSON sidecars. PNG + SVG + JSON sidecar. Matplotlib + seaborn + specialized libs. Config-driven (metric/loss names from config, never hardcoded). Output to config.output_dir / figures/.

task.name: Figure generation engine
task.description:
generate_figures(db_path, pairwise, variance, rankings, config, output_dir) -> list[FigureArtifact].
Tier 1 (must-have): F1 raincloud (ptitprince), F2 effect size heatmap (seaborn), F3 CD diagram (scikit-posthocs), F4 forest plot (matplotlib), F5 training curves. Tier 2 (should-have): F6 fold stability, F7 metric correlation, F8 DABEST.
Color: Okabe-Ito palette. Export: PNG (300 DPI) + SVG + JSON sidecar. rcParams: font.size=9, pdf.fonttype=42, dpi=300.
All output to output_dir/figures/ (config-driven, Docker volume-mounted).

task.name: JSON sidecar schema and writer
task.description: FigureSidecar dataclass: figure_id, title, generated_at, git_commit, software_versions, source (duckdb_artifact, query, run_ids, experiment), data_summary, data (per-condition arrays), statistical_tests. write_sidecar(sidecar, path) -> Path. Self-contained: re-plottable without DuckDB.

task.name: LaTeX table generation
task.description: generate_tables(pairwise, variance, rankings, config, output_dir) -> list[TableArtifact]. 5 tables: T1 main comparison, T2 p-value matrix, T3 effect sizes, T4 variance decomposition, T5 champion summary. booktabs, textbf for best values. Output to output_dir/tables/. Reuses format_comparison_latex() pattern from comparison.py.

task.name: Lineage manifest generation
task.description: build_lineage_manifest(manifest, config, figures, tables) -> dict. Schema: schema_version, biostatistics_run_id, generated_at, fingerprint, git_commit, environment, source_experiments, dataset_provenance, tripod_ai_items (9 items), artifacts_produced, statistical_methods.

task.name: MLflow run logging
task.description: log_biostatistics_run(manifest, db_path, figures, tables, pairwise, variance, lineage) -> str (run_id). Creates ONE mlrun in 'minivess_biostatistics' experiment. Logs: biostatistics.duckdb, parquet/, figures/, sidecars/, tables/, lineage.json. Tags: upstream_fingerprint, n_source_runs, n_conditions, n_metrics.

phase.name: Flow Assembly
phase.description: CRITICAL PHASE: This is where the flow code lives. It MUST import from prefect directly (NOT _prefect_compat). It MUST call _require_docker_context() at entry. The guardrail tests from Phase 0 (task 0.3) MUST pass.

task.name: Prefect flow assembly
task.description:
IMPORTS: from prefect import flow, task (DIRECT — no _prefect_compat)
_require_docker_context() check at flow entry: - Check Path("/.dockerenv").exists() - Escape hatch: os.environ.get("MINIVESS_TESTING") == "1" - RuntimeError with actionable message if outside Docker
@flow(name="minivess-biostatistics") def run_biostatistics_flow(config_path: str = "/app/configs/biostatistics/default.yaml", trigger_source: str = "manual") -> BiostatisticsResult: _require_docker_context() config = load_config(config_path) manifest = task_discover_source_runs(...) validation = task_validate_source_completeness(...) db_path = task_build_biostatistics_duckdb(...) pairwise = task_compute_pairwise_comparisons(...) # parallel with next variance = task_compute_variance_decomposition(...) # parallel with prev rankings = task_compute_rankings(...) figures = task_generate_figures(...) tables = task_generate_latex_tables(...) run_id = task_log_biostatistics_run(...) return BiostatisticsResult(...)
Fingerprint-based short-circuit: skip if source runs haven't changed.
GUARDRAIL TESTS FROM PHASE 0 MUST NOW PASS: - test_no_compat_layer_import -> GREEN - test_docker_context_check_present -> GREEN - test_flow_imports_real_prefect -> GREEN
task.tests: - test_flow_runs_with_mock_data (MINIVESS_TESTING=1) - test_flow_produces_all_artifact_types - test_flow_short_circuits_on_same_fingerprint - test_flow_raises_on_validation_failure - test_flow_raises_outside_docker (no MINIVESS_TESTING, no /.dockerenv)

task.name: Trigger chain integration
task.description: Add "biostatistics" to _DEFAULT_FLOWS after "analyze" (best-effort position). Update flow count in tests. Verify chain order.

task.name: Full integration test
task.description: Full pipeline test with synthetic mlruns: 1. Create synthetic mlruns structure (4 conditions x 3 folds x 70 volumes) 2. Set MINIVESS_TESTING=1 (test escape hatch) 3. Run full flow 4. Validate ALL output artifacts: - biostatistics.duckdb exists with 7 tables - 8+ PNG figures + 8+ JSON sidecars - 5+ LaTeX table files - lineage.json with correct fingerprint - MLflow run in minivess_biostatistics experiment 5. Verify fingerprint-based short-circuit (run twice, second should skip)

guardrail.description: AST test: biostatistics_flow.py does not import _prefect_compat
Glob test: no scripts/*biostatistic* files exist
Grep test: no PREFECT_DISABLED in biostatistics .sh scripts
YAML parse: docker-compose has biostatistics service
AST test: no tempfile import or /tmp string in biostatistics code
AST test: flow file imports from prefect package
Runtime test: flow raises RuntimeError without Docker or TESTING env

section.name: stochastic
section.enforcement: documentation-level, AI may bypass under pressure

guardrail.description: This XML plan references all 6 fuckup metalearning docs

environment.name: staging
environment.description: Docker Compose on local machine with GPU

environment.name: pytest
environment.description: Test suite ONLY — not a run environment
environment.constraint: Tests use tmp_path fixtures, never repo-relative paths
Tests mock MLflow, never create real runs
Tests DO NOT use PREFECT_DISABLED — they use MINIVESS_TESTING

banned.name: dev-local
banned.reason: No 'quick local run' bypassing Docker. CLAUDE.md rule #17.

banned.name: test-hack
banned.reason: No scripts/run_biostatistics.py. No uv run python.

execution-protocol.rule: Each task follows TDD: RED -> GREEN -> VERIFY -> FIX -> CHECKPOINT -> CONVERGE
Commit after each task passes. Message: "feat(biostatistics): Task X.Y — description"
If a task fails, do NOT move to next task. Fix first.
Run full test suite after each phase completes.
Before implementing any task, re-read task description AND relevant section of docs/planning/biostatistics-prefect-flow.md.
Statistical functions have ZERO Prefect dependency. Only Phase 8 imports @flow/@task.
Never hardcode metric/loss/experiment names. Everything config-driven.
New dependencies: run "uv add {package}" BEFORE writing import statements.
Resume protocol: read this XML, check git log, resume from next task.
NEVER create scripts/run_biostatistics*.py or any standalone entry point.
NEVER import from _prefect_compat in biostatistics flow code.
NEVER use tempfile or /tmp for biostatistics outputs.
NEVER set PREFECT_DISABLED=1 in biostatistics-related code (except pytest mocks).
After Phase 8 is implemented, verify ALL Phase 0 guardrail tests pass.
Docker infrastructure (Phase 0) MUST be committed before flow code (Phase 8).
The test escape hatch is MINIVESS_TESTING=1 (NOT PREFECT_DISABLED=1).

issue-template.title: biostatistics: Task {id} — {name}

issue.title: biostatistics: Task 0.2 — Deployment config (Prefect + image map)
biostatistics: Task 0.4 — Add dependencies to pyproject.toml
biostatistics: Task 2.1 — Extended DuckDB schema + extraction
biostatistics: Task 3.1 — Cliff's delta + VDA + CI fix
biostatistics: Task 3.2 — Pairwise comparisons from DuckDB
biostatistics: Task 3.3 — Bayesian signed-rank with ROPE
biostatistics: Task 5.2 — JSON sidecar schema and writer
biostatistics: Task 8.1 — Prefect flow assembly (NO compat layer)

verification.command: uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run pytest tests/ -x -q && uv run ruff check src/ tests/ && uv run mypy src/

ref.lesson: Web-search models beyond knowledge cutoff before implementing
Write requested artifacts to disk using Write tool
Never bypass pre-commit hooks — surface failures to user
Silent fallback = lie. Hard-fail with actionable error messages
Training MUST go through Prefect flows in Docker. No shortcuts.
No regex for structured data. Use ast.parse, json.loads, str.split.
Docker infrastructure exists. USE IT. Don't bypass it.
Every compat/stub layer that silently degrades will mask critical failures.

references.ref: Nadeau-Bengio variance correction for K-fold CV
ICC guideline: ICC(2,1) for cross-fold consistency
TRIPOD+AI 27-item checklist for AI model reporting
Data provenance failures in clinical ML
